```{r}
  select(Position, n_players, low_speed_zone, moderate_zone, 
         high_speed_zone, sprint_zone, max_sprint_zone)

print(recommendations)

```

Now we can look at a simple table showing high speed and sprint cutoffs

```{r}
simple_thresholds <- position_thresholds %>%
  mutate(
    high_speed_cutoff = paste0(round(high_speed_threshold, 1), " MPH (match-based 70th %ile)"),
    sprint_cutoff = paste0(round(sprint_threshold, 1), " MPH (match-based 90th %ile)")
  ) %>%
  select(Position, n_players, high_speed_cutoff, sprint_cutoff)

print(simple_thresholds)

```


Now we can compare the old and new thresholds. First we'll create a zone comparison dataset to plot 
```{r}
zone_comparison <- threshold_comparison %>%
  select(Position, 
         `OLD: Arbitrary 70%` = arbitrary_hs_avg,
         `NEW: Match-Based 70th` = high_speed_threshold,
         `OLD: Arbitrary 90%` = arbitrary_sprint_avg,
         `NEW: Match-Based 90th` = sprint_threshold) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Position, names_to = "Method", values_to = "Threshold_MPH") %>%
  mutate(
    Type = ifelse(grepl("70", Method), "High-Speed", "Sprint"),
    Approach = ifelse(grepl("OLD", Method), "Old (Arbitrary)", "New (Match-Based Position-Specific)")
  )

```



Now plot
```{r}
ggplot(zone_comparison, aes(x = Position, y = Threshold_MPH, fill = Approach)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~Type, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Old (Arbitrary)" = "darkred", 
                                "New (Match-Based Position-Specific)" = "forestgreen")) +
  labs(
    title = "Recommended Velocity Zone Thresholds",
    subtitle = "Old arbitrary (70%/90% of max) vs new match-based position-specific percentiles",
    x = "Position",
    y = "Velocity Threshold (MPH)",
    fill = "Method"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("OldvsNew.png", width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300)

```


Now I can create a comprehensive summary of the analysis

```{r}

#calculate effect size from regular ANOVA for reference
ss_between <- summary(regular_anova)[[1]]["Position", "Sum Sq"]
ss_total <- sum(summary(regular_anova)[[1]][, "Sum Sq"])
eta_squared <- ss_between / ss_total

comprehensive_summary <- data.frame(
  Question = c(
    "Position-specific profiles in matches?",
    "How much faster than arbitrary?",
    "Players misclassified?",
    "High-intensity % in training?",
    "Recommended approach?"
  ),
  
  Finding = c(
    paste0("MIXED (Welch's p=", round(welch_anova$p.value, 3), 
           ", Kruskal-Wallis p=", round(kruskal_test$p.value, 3), 
           ", η²=", round(eta_squared, 3), ")"),
    paste0(round(gap_summary$avg_gap_70th_pct, 1), "% (70th) and ",
           round(gap_summary$avg_gap_90th_pct, 1), "% (90th) higher"),
    paste0(round(overall_misclass$hs_error_rate, 1), "% (", 
           overall_misclass$hs_misclassified, "/", 
           overall_misclass$total_players, " players)"),
    paste0(round(team_velocity_summary$avg_high_intensity, 1), 
           "% of distance >12 MPH"),
    "Use match-based position-specific 70th/90th percentiles"
  ),
  
  Implication = c(
    "Statistical evidence is mixed due to variance heterogeneity, but practical differences in positional match demands justify position-specific thresholds",
    "Arbitrary thresholds severely underestimate match-competitive intensity across all positions",
    "Arbitrary approach fails to identify match-ready vs developing athletes within positions",
    "Minimal game-speed exposure in training makes proper threshold definition very important for competition readiness",
    "Replace arbitrary % of max with match-based position-specific percentiles"
  )
)

print(comprehensive_summary)
```

### Issues with Current Velocity Zone Classification

Current velocity zone classification relies on arbitrary percentage-based thresholds, which are high-speed high speed work at >= 70% of a player's all-time maximum velocity and sprinting at >= 90% of all-time maximum velocity. While this approach appears individualized, it fails to distinguish between fast and slow players within positions. 

By definition, every player can achieve 70% and 90% of their own maximum velocity, meaning the arbitrary method classifies 100% of athletes as high-speed and sprint capable regardless of their actual speed relative to positional peers. This produces a 27.8 and 50 percent misclassification rate for high speed and sprint work respectably, with 15 players falsely labeled as high-speed capable and 27 falsely labeled as sprint capable when compared to match based position specific performance benchmarks. The arbitrary approach sets standards far lower than match work indicates. 


### Match Based Position Specific Percentile Thresholds

Analysis of 67 validated matches (2023-2025) shows that match based position specific percentile thresholds give a more valid and actionable alternative. Using the 70th percentile of match maximum velocities for high-speed work and the 90th percentile for sprint work, position-specific cutoffs are higher than arbitrary thresholds. These thresholds are 3-5.2 MPH higher (+25-41%) for high-speed and 0.5-2.1 MPH higher (+3-13%) for sprint work, with Goal Keepers showing the lowest values and Forwards showing the highest.

Position differences in match based maximum velocity were checked using Welch's ANOVA (which does not assume equal variances) because of different variances across positions (variance ratio = 8.41, Levene's test p < 0.05). Results were marginally non-significant for the Welch's test, though Kruskal-Wallis test on medians suggested some positional variation. While the statistical evidence is mixed, practical differences in positional match demands, along with with the group sponsor's endorsement to complement already existing zones justify position-specific thresholds for competition-focused load monitoring.

### Why Match-Based Thresholds?

Match based position specific percentile thresholds work where arbitrary thresholds don't for three reasons:

1. Ecological Validity: Training sessions inherently have constraints that limit maximum velocity during a session. Small sided games restrict space, drills help skills, and recovery sessions limit intensity. Match data isolates the true competitive capability of the athletes because matches provide consistent and genuine opportunities for maximum effort. 

2. Competition-Focused Benchmarking: Training prepares athletes for competition, so thresholds should reflect the real competitive demands. Match-based cutoffs make sure that load monitoring answers the question: "Are we exposing players to game-level intensity?" Using the 70th and 90th percentiles of match velocities identifies the top 30% (high-speed) and top 10% (sprint) of competitive performances within each position. The position split provides a meaningful distinction that differentiates match-ready athletes from athletes who are still developing competitive speed abilities.

3. Important for Low-Intensity Programs: High intensity training exposure is very low across all positions (0.9-2.7% of total distance >12 MPH), with the remaining ~97.3% of training occurring at speeds below 12 MPH. With so little game speed work already occurring in training, correct classification of what match-intensity means becomes very important for ensuring that the limited high speed exposure is correctly monitored.

### Practical Application

This analysis supports using match based position specific velocity zones based on these thresholds:

Defenders: High-speed >= 17.7 MPH, Sprint 18.2 >= MPH
Forwards: High-speed >= 17.7 MPH, Sprint 19 >= MPH
Goal Keepers: High-speed 15 >=  MPH, Sprint 15.9 ≥ MPH
Midfielders: High-speed >= 17.8 MPH, Sprint 18.4 >= MPH


These cutoffs support the arbitrary 70%/90% of max approach with derived benchmarks that reflect legitimate competitive performance distributions within each position. Using match based position specific standards makes sure that velocity zone classifications are based on game demands rather than training artifacts. 


Benefits for the Change:

More accurate talent identification: Identifying the fastest 10-30% of players per position based on performance
Better load management: Prescribing correct high-speed exposure relative to competitive demands (ex., "Forwards accumulated 85% of position-appropriate match high-speed distance this week")
Improved performance monitoring: Tracking changes in high speed running relative to competition specific benchmarks


### Key Takeaways From The Analysis

Match based position specific percentile thresholds create distinctions between match ready and developing athletes within each position, setting standards 25-42% higher for high-speed work and 4-14% higher for sprint work compared to the arbitrary approach.

Despite mixed statistical evidence for positional differences in max game speed (Welch's ANOVA p = 0.056), match-based position-specific thresholds are justified by 
(1) practical differences in positional match demands and roles 
(2) ecological validity of competition based benchmarks
(3) endorsement from sponsor to complement pre-existing zones 
(4) better use for individualized load monitoring and talent identification

This recommendation is also helped by the extremely low exposure to high intensity training (0.9-2.7% of total distance), making threshold definition incredibly important for optimizing the limited game speed work that does occur and making sure that athletes are prepared for competitive match intensity.






#___________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

## Flow Chart


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(grid)
library(gridExtra)
# Create flowchart with full descriptions
ggplot() +
  
  # ROW 1 BOXES (Left to Right)
  # Box 1: Raw Data
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.6, xmax = 1.4, ymin = 1.7, ymax = 2.3,
           fill = '#E8F4F8', color = "black", size = 1.2) +
  annotate("text", x = 1, y = 2.15, 
           label = "Raw Data Sources\n(2023-2025)", 
           size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 1, y = 1.9, 
           label = "• Catapult GPS\n  (~23,696 sessions)\n• Match schedules (70 games)\n• Player stats", 
           size = 3, lineheight = 0.9) +
  
  # Box 2: Standardization
  annotate("rect", xmin = 1.6, xmax = 2.4, ymin = 1.7, ymax = 2.3,
           fill = '#B3D9FF', color = "black", size = 1.2) +
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = 2.15, 
           label = "Data\nStandardization", 
           size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = 1.9, 
           label = "• Column names normalized\n• Date/numeric parsing\n• Missing fields dropped", 
           size = 3, lineheight = 0.9) +
  
  # Box 3: Position Backfilling
  annotate("rect", xmin = 2.6, xmax = 3.4, ymin = 1.7, ymax = 2.3,
           fill = '#FFD9B3', color = "black", size = 1.2) +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 2.15, 
           label = "Position\nBackfilling", 
           size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 1.9, 
           label = "• 6,735 missing positions\n• Player history lookup\n• 100% recovery", 
           size = 3, lineheight = 0.9) +
  
  # ROW 2 BOXES (Right to Left)
  # Box 4: Quality Filtering
  annotate("rect", xmin = 2.6, xmax = 3.4, ymin = 0.7, ymax = 1.3,
           fill = '#FFB3BA', color = "black", size = 1.2) +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 1.15, 
           label = "Quality Filtering\n& Merging", 
           size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 0.9, 
           label = "• GPS errors removed\n• Defender positions consolidated\n• Intensity metrics", 
           size = 3, lineheight = 0.9) +
  
  # Box 5: Integration
  annotate("rect", xmin = 1.6, xmax = 2.4, ymin = 0.7, ymax = 1.3,
           fill = '#D4EDC9', color = "black", size = 1.2) +
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = 1.15, 
           label = "Integration &\nFeature Engineering", 
           size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = 0.9, 
           label = "• Join Catapult + Schedule\n• Calculate MD-, EWMA, ACWR\n• Classify phases", 
           size = 3, lineheight = 0.9) +
  
  # Box 6: Final Output
  annotate("rect", xmin = 0.6, xmax = 1.4, ymin = 0.7, ymax = 1.3,
           fill = '#90EE90', color = "black", size = 1.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 1, y = 1.15, 
           label = "Analysis-Ready\nDatasets", 
           size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 1, y = 0.9, 
           label = "• Session-level (16,800)\n• Match-level stats\n• 4 positions filled", 
           size = 3, lineheight = 0.9) +
  
  # ARROWS - Row 1 (left to right)
  annotate("segment", x = 1.4, xend = 1.6, y = 2, yend = 2,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm"), type = "closed"), 
           size = 1.2, color = "black") +
  annotate("segment", x = 2.4, xend = 2.6, y = 2, yend = 2,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm"), type = "closed"), 
           size = 1.2, color = "black") +
  
  # ARROW - Down from backfill to quality
  annotate("segment", x = 3, xend = 3, y = 1.7, yend = 1.3,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm"), type = "closed"), 
           size = 1.2, color = "black") +
  
  # ARROWS - Row 2 (right to left)
  annotate("segment", x = 2.6, xend = 2.4, y = 1, yend = 1,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm"), type = "closed"), 
           size = 1.2, color = "black") +
  annotate("segment", x = 1.6, xend = 1.4, y = 1, yend = 1,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm"), type = "closed"), 
           size = 1.2, color = "black") +
  
  # Formatting
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1) +
  theme_void() +
  xlim(0.4, 3.6) +
  ylim(0.5, 2.5)

ggsave("flowchart_cleaning.png", width = 14, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```





```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# SLIDE 2 VISUAL: WHY ARBITRARY THRESHOLDS DON'T WORK
# ==============================================================================

# Create example data showing the problem
arbitrary_problem <- data.frame(
  Player = c("Player A\n(Slow)", "Player B\n(Average)", "Player C\n(Fast)"),
  Max_Velocity = c(15, 18, 21),
  Arbitrary_70 = c(15 * 0.70, 18 * 0.70, 21 * 0.70),
  Arbitrary_90 = c(15 * 0.90, 18 * 0.90, 21 * 0.90)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Player = factor(Player, levels = c("Player A\n(Slow)", "Player B\n(Average)", "Player C\n(Fast)"))
  )

# Reshape for plotting
arbitrary_problem_long <- arbitrary_problem %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Max_Velocity, Arbitrary_70, Arbitrary_90),
    names_to = "Threshold_Type",
    values_to = "Velocity"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Threshold_Type = factor(Threshold_Type, 
                           levels = c("Max_Velocity", "Arbitrary_70", "Arbitrary_90"),
                           labels = c("All-Time Max", "High-Speed\n(70% of max)", "Sprint\n(90% of max)"))
  )

ggplot(arbitrary_problem_long, aes(x = Player, y = Velocity, fill = Threshold_Type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Velocity, 1), " MPH")), 
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), 
            vjust = -0.3, size = 4.5, fontface = "bold") +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "All-Time Max" = "#000000",              # Black
    "High-Speed\n(70% of max)" = "#DC4405",  # Orange/Red
    "Sprint\n(90% of max)" = "#CFB87C"       # CU Gold
  )) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Arbitrary Thresholds Don't Identify Fast Players",
    x = "",
    y = "Velocity (MPH)",
    fill = "Threshold Type"
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 23), breaks = seq(0, 22, by = 2)) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )


```


```{r}

# Use your existing velocity_bands data
bands_for_slide <- velocity_bands %>%
  select(Position, band1_pct, band2_pct, band3_pct, band4_pct) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("band"), names_to = "band", values_to = "percentage") %>%
  mutate(
    # REVERSE the factor levels for stacking order
    band = factor(band, 
                  levels = c("band4_pct", "band3_pct", "band2_pct", "band1_pct"),  # Band 4 first = stacks last (right side)
                  labels = c("Band 4 (14.4+ MPH)\nSprint",
                            "Band 3 (12-14.4 MPH)\nHigh-Speed", 
                            "Band 2 (9.6-12 MPH)\nModerate",
                            "Band 1 (0-9.6 MPH)\nLow-Speed")),
    Position = factor(Position, levels = c("Forward", "Midfielder", "Defender", "Goal Keeper"))
  )

# Calculate average percentages for each band
band_averages <- bands_for_slide %>%
  group_by(band) %>%
  summarise(avg_pct = mean(percentage), .groups = "drop")

# Create new labels with percentages - REVERSED ORDER FOR LEGEND
bands_for_slide <- bands_for_slide %>%
  left_join(band_averages, by = "band") %>%
  mutate(
    band_with_pct = paste0(as.character(band), " [", round(avg_pct, 1), "%]"),
    band_with_pct = factor(band_with_pct, 
                          levels = c(
                            paste0("Band 1 (0-9.6 MPH)\nLow-Speed [", round(band_averages$avg_pct[band_averages$band == "Band 1 (0-9.6 MPH)\nLow-Speed"], 1), "%]"),
                            paste0("Band 2 (9.6-12 MPH)\nModerate [", round(band_averages$avg_pct[band_averages$band == "Band 2 (9.6-12 MPH)\nModerate"], 1), "%]"),
                            paste0("Band 3 (12-14.4 MPH)\nHigh-Speed [", round(band_averages$avg_pct[band_averages$band == "Band 3 (12-14.4 MPH)\nHigh-Speed"], 1), "%]"),
                            paste0("Band 4 (14.4+ MPH)\nSprint [", round(band_averages$avg_pct[band_averages$band == "Band 4 (14.4+ MPH)\nSprint"], 1), "%]")
                          ))
  )

# Get the actual levels for scale_fill_manual
legend_levels <- levels(bands_for_slide$band_with_pct)

ggplot(bands_for_slide, aes(x = percentage, y = Position, fill = band_with_pct)) +
  geom_col(position = position_stack(reverse = TRUE), width = 0.7) +  # ADD reverse = TRUE
  
  # Show labels only for larger bands
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(percentage >= 3, paste0(round(percentage, 1), "%"), "")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5, reverse = TRUE),  # ADD reverse = TRUE
            size = 4.5, color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  
  # Colors in order: Band 1, Band 2, Band 3, Band 4
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = setNames(
      c("#8B9DC3", "#DEB887", "#F4A460", "#DC143C"),
      legend_levels
    )
  ) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Training Load Heavily Skewed Toward Low-Intensity Work",
    x = "Percentage of Total Distance (%)",
    y = "",
    fill = "Velocity Band [Avg %]"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9, lineheight = 0.9),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

ggsave("velocity_band_distribution_reversed_fixed.png", width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
# Define parameters for a smooth normal distribution
mean_val <- 18
sd_val <- 2

# Calculate percentiles from theoretical normal distribution
p70 <- qnorm(0.70, mean = mean_val, sd = sd_val)
p90 <- qnorm(0.90, mean = mean_val, sd = sd_val)

# Create smooth x values
x_vals <- seq(mean_val - 4*sd_val, mean_val + 4*sd_val, length.out = 1000)

# Create data frame for plotting
smooth_dist <- data.frame(
  x = x_vals,
  y = dnorm(x_vals, mean = mean_val, sd = sd_val)
)

# Get max density for label positioning
max_density <- dnorm(mean_val, mean = mean_val, sd = sd_val)

ggplot(smooth_dist, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  
  # Below 70th percentile (gray)
  geom_area(data = smooth_dist %>% filter(x < p70),
            fill = "#A2A4A3", alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # 70th to 90th percentile (green - high-speed)
  geom_area(data = smooth_dist %>% filter(x >= p70 & x < p90),
            fill = "#4A7729", alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # Above 90th percentile (gold - sprint)
  geom_area(data = smooth_dist %>% filter(x >= p90),
            fill = "#CFB87C", alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # Smooth outline
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = "#000000") +
  
  # Threshold lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = p70, color = "#4A7729", linewidth = 1.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = p90, color = "#CFB87C", linewidth = 1.8, linetype = "dashed") +
  
  # Labels at TOP - MORE separation
  annotate("label", x = p70, y = max_density * 1.15, 
           label = "70th Percentile\n(High-Speed)", 
           color = "white", fill = "#4A7729", fontface = "bold", size = 4.5, 
           hjust = 0.5, label.padding = unit(0.5, "lines")) +
  
  annotate("label", x = p90, y = max_density * 1.45, 
           label = "90th Percentile\n(Sprint)", 
           color = "white", fill = "#CFB87C", fontface = "bold", size = 4.5, 
           hjust = 0.5, label.padding = unit(0.5, "lines")) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Maximum Velocity (MPH)",
    y = "",
    caption = "Example distribution: Midfielder match maximum velocities"
  ) +
  
  # Expand y-axis to make room for top labels
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, max_density * 1.6)) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray50", 
                                 margin = margin(t = 10)),
    plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20)
  )

ggsave("percentile_smooth_normal.png", width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```




```{r}

# Prepare table data from threshold_comparison
threshold_table <- threshold_comparison %>%
  select(Position, high_speed_threshold, sprint_threshold, n_players) %>%
  arrange(Position)

# Add sample sizes from match data
sample_sizes <- player_max_velocities_match %>%
  group_by(Position) %>%
  summarise(n_obs = n(), .groups = "drop")

threshold_table <- threshold_table %>%
  left_join(sample_sizes, by = "Position") %>%
  mutate(
    Position = factor(Position, levels = c("Defender", "Forward", "Goal Keeper", "Midfielder")),
    high_speed_threshold = round(high_speed_threshold, 1),
    sprint_threshold = round(sprint_threshold, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Position)

# Convert to long format for ggplot table
table_long <- threshold_table %>%
  mutate(
    Position_Label = as.character(Position),
    HS_Label = paste0(high_speed_threshold, " MPH"),
    Sprint_Label = paste0(sprint_threshold, " MPH"),
    Sample_Label = paste0(n_obs, " players")  # CHANGED: "obs" → "players"
  ) %>%
  select(Position_Label, HS_Label, Sprint_Label, Sample_Label) %>%
  mutate(row_id = row_number()) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -row_id, names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric, 
                   levels = c("Position_Label", "HS_Label", "Sprint_Label", "Sample_Label"),
                   labels = c("Position", "High-Speed\n(70th %ile)", "Sprint\n(90th %ile)", "Sample Size")),
    row_id = factor(row_id, levels = 4:1)  # Reverse order for top-to-bottom
  )

# Create table visualization
ggplot(table_long, aes(x = Metric, y = row_id, label = Value)) +
  geom_tile(fill = "white", color = "#000000", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_text(size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  scale_y_discrete(breaks = NULL) +
  labs(
    title = "Recommended Position-Specific Velocity Thresholds",
    subtitle = "Based on 67 validated matches (2023-2025)",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40", margin = margin(b = 20)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20)
  )

ggsave("recommended_thresholds_table.png", width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```
```{r}
names(threshold_comparison)
```




```{r}
# Filter for 2024 only
monthly_2024 <- monthly_by_year %>%
  filter(year == 2024)

# Create the plot
ggplot(monthly_2024, 
       aes(x = factor(month_name, levels = month.abb), 
           y = avg_load, 
           group = 1)) +
  
  # Phase shading - extended to boundaries
  annotate("rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = 6, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, 
           alpha = 0.05, fill = "lightblue") +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 6, xmax = 8, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, 
           alpha = 0.05, fill = "orange") +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 8, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, 
           alpha = 0.05, fill = "darkred") +
  
  # Phase labels
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 100, 
           label = "OFF-SEASON", size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 7, y = 100, 
           label = "PRE", size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 9.5, y = 100, 
           label = "IN-SEASON", size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  
  # Line and points
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5, color = "#457B9D", alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 4, color = "#457B9D") +
  
  # Phase dividers
  geom_vline(xintercept = 6, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 8, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  
  # Value labels
  geom_text(aes(label = round(avg_load, 0)), 
            vjust = -1.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold", color = "#457B9D") +
  
  scale_x_discrete(limits = month.abb[1:11]) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 450), breaks = seq(0, 450, by = 50)) +
  
  labs(
    title = "2024 Monthly Training Load Progression",
    subtitle = "Average load across seasonal phases (January - November, no Jan/May/Jun/Dec data)",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Average Total Player Load (AU)"
  ) +
  
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

ggsave("monthly_load_2024.png", width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```
